title: Nutrition and Physical Fitness
content: This open textbook integrates foundational nutrition science, physical activity principles, and practical sports nutrition for active lifestyles. Core topics include energy balance, carbohydrate use in exercise, protein estimation, body composition, and long-term weight management strategies.

title: Nutrition and Physical Fitness
content: The framework emphasizes balancing energy intake and expenditure for different goals: reducing body fat, increasing muscle mass, or maintaining weight. It also highlights practical methods for estimating intake and expenditure and adjusting plans with behavior-level strategies.

title: Nutrition and Physical Fitness
content: Protein-related chapters cover protein function, digestion and absorption, dietary sources, and approaches to estimating needs for active populations. It also discusses supplementation and how to evaluate claims against evidence-based guidance.

title: Open Textbook of Exercise Physiology (OTEP)
content: OTEP is an open textbook project organized to cover the standard range of exercise physiology topics from fundamentals to applied performance and special populations. It is edited by Brian R. MacIntosh and includes chapters by multiple domain experts.

title: Open Textbook of Exercise Physiology (OTEP)
content: The table of contents includes nervous system function, muscle structure and contraction, energy supply, pulmonary and circulatory responses, homeostasis, metabolic measurement, nutrition and energy balance, body composition, fatigue, and molecular adaptations to training.

title: The Physiology of Exercise (3rd Edition)
content: This open textbook is positioned for upper-level exercise science learners and covers metabolism, energy expenditure, and integrated system responses to exercise, including nervous, cardiovascular, endocrine, muscular, and respiratory domains. Chapters are structured with learning objectives and key terms for applied understanding.
